action: Turn the decision-event validator into an enforceable repository gate and capture the first observed runtime verification without rewriting earlier uncertainty.

observed: Five adversarial Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using logic-equivalent source reconstructed from the committed files.
The reconstructed ledger validator returned valid=true with zero errors for the observed ledger structure.
A GitHub Actions workflow now runs both commands on relevant pushes, pull requests, and manual dispatches.
resolved: The M-004 runtime-verification handoff now has an observed local execution and a committed CI enforcement route.
unresolved: No GitHub Actions run result for workflow commit 9c4702635a275b1580776205fe5e099e733367da was observed during this run.
Byte-identical execution of the committed files was not available because the local runtime could not clone GitHub; CI remains the authoritative byte-identical verification route.
M-002 through M-005 remain absent from the shared ACTIVE_QUEUE aggregate and still require conflict-safe reconciliation.

verification.runtime: isolated Node runtime using reconstructed logic-equivalent source
verification.falsification_route: Any nonzero exit from either command or any failed GitHub Actions job falsifies the claim that the decision ledger satisfies the committed contract.

privacy_boundary: Only public repository source, commit metadata, and non-sensitive validation output were used. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, contact data, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
handoff: Observe the first GitHub Actions result for the continuity workflow, then reconcile M-002 through M-005 into ACTIVE_QUEUE.json only after fetching the latest aggregate blob and preserving every sidecar reference.
